title: FIFA World Cup Group A Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group A in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mexico's co-host advantage, including the Group A opener against South Africa at Estadio Azteca on June 11 and additional matches in Mexico City and Guadalajara, combined with draws versus Portugal and Belgium in early April friendlies, positions them as trader consensus favorite at 46.5% to advance from the group stage. Czechia's UEFA playoff heroics—capped by a penalty win over Denmark—and qualifiers' best defensive record among contenders fuel their 23.5% implied probability, edging South Korea (21.5%), whose counter-attacking qualifiers shone but recent losses to Austria and Ivory Coast tempered optimism around Son Heung-min's form. South Africa lags at 5.3% despite solid organization under Hugo Broos, with all squads reporting peak fitness in late-April pre-camps amid statistical spikes like Mexico's 18% improved transition speed.

eventMetadata.context_description: Switzerland leads trader consensus at 52.5% implied probability to win FIFA World Cup 2026 Group B, driven by their consistent round-of-16 appearances in four of the last five tournaments, elite defensive organization from UEFA qualifiers, and Granit Xhaka's midfield control, positioning them as the squad with clearest path to top the group alongside Canada, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Qatar. Canada's 25% share reflects co-host home advantage across all matches in Toronto and Vancouver, bolstered by Alphonso Davies' pace and recent friendly form, though historical group-stage exits temper expectations. Bosnia's 21.5% surged after their March 31 playoff penalty shootout victory over Italy to qualify, highlighting physicality and set-piece threat for potential upsets. Qatar trails at 2%, hampered by weaker recent AFC results and long travel. Pre-tournament camps show all squads sharpening tactics amid no major injury disruptions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Brazil's overwhelming 75% implied probability as Group C winner stems from their unmatched squad depth and attacking firepower under Carlo Ancelotti, bolstered by recent squad announcements integrating young stars like Endrick while prioritizing fitness in New Jersey training camps. Morocco's 17.5% reflects their elite defensive organization from 2022 semifinal run and strong African qualifiers, positioning them as the prime second-place contender with finalized backline under coach Mohamed Ouahbi. Trader consensus highlights Brazil's historical group dominance and no major injuries shifting dynamics ahead of June fixtures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices USA at 35.5% and Türkiye at 34.0% to win Group D, capturing a fiercely competitive group where the USMNT's home advantage across multiple 2026 World Cup venues is tempered by Türkiye's momentum from a gritty UEFA playoff run—capped by a 1-0 win over Kosovo on March 31—and a recent 2-1 friendly upset over the USA. Paraguay's 17.5% implied probability stems from their resilient CONMEBOL qualifying finish and a 1-0 friendly win over Greece in March, positioning them as live contenders despite a No. 40 FIFA ranking. Australia's 6.9% trails amid softer recent FIFA Series triumphs over Cameroon and Curaçao, underscoring the Socceroos' underdog status against higher-ranked foes (USA 16th, Türkiye 22nd, Australia 27th). USMNT stumbles in March friendlies versus Portugal and Belgium have kept this race tight ahead of the June group stage opener.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany's superior squad depth, featuring stars like Jamal Musiala and Joshua Kimmich, combined with elite pressing and possession dominance from recent European qualifiers, drives their 72.5% implied probability as Group E winners, reflecting trader consensus on their historical group-stage command despite past early exits. Late-April training camps at premium U.S. bases report full squads and glowing fitness updates, stabilizing odds absent injury disruptions.

title: Which continent will win the 2026 Fifa World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Europe at 72.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by UEFA's unmatched depth with top FIFA-ranked teams like France (No. 1), Spain (No. 2), England (No. 4), Portugal (No. 5), and Netherlands (No. 7) all qualified via dominant European qualifiers. South America's 20.5% reflects defending champions Argentina (No. 3) and Brazil (No. 6), bolstered by CONMEBOL securing six strong slots. Lower odds for Africa (2.8%), Asia (2.1%), North America (2.1%), and Oceania (0.3%) stem from limited knockout pedigree despite expanded slots and qualifiers like Morocco's rise. Recent March playoffs saw UEFA upsets with Italy eliminated but Sweden, Bosnia, Czech Republic, and Turkey advancing, while final draw groups set favorable paths for European powerhouses ahead of the June 11 kickoff.

title: World Cup game relocated away from Mexico?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if it is announced that any 2026 FIFA World Cup match originally scheduled to be held in Mexico will be relocated to a location outside of Mexico by June 10, 2026, 11:59 ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying announcement will immediately resolve the market to "Yes" regardless of whether the relocation is later revoked.

The resolution source will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: FIFA's late March 2026 release of the full World Cup match schedule confirmed Mexico's three venues—Estadio Azteca, Guadalajara, and Monterrey—for 13 fixtures, including the June 11 opener pitting host Mexico against South Africa, locking in assignments with the tournament six weeks away. The recent reinauguration of Estadio Azteca and completed renovations across sites, absent any security disruptions or infrastructure shortfalls, underpin trader consensus implying 95% probability against relocation. This reflects crowd wisdom on finalized logistics and FIFA's commitment to the tri-nation host setup. Realistic shifts would require unprecedented events like cartel violence escalation or pitch failures prompting emergency venue swaps to U.S. or Canadian stadiums.

title: Which team will replace Iran at FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve in favor of the men’s national team that is confirmed by FIFA to replace the Football Federation Islamic Republic of Iran (IR) at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If an unlisted men’s national team is officially announced to replace Iran at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, this market will resolve to “Other”

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is no confirmation that any team will replace Iran within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “No Replacement”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no replacement for Iran at the 2026 FIFA World Cup (71%) following official dismissals of a US envoy's recent proposal to award their AFC slot to Italy, which failed UEFA playoffs and rejected the idea as "impossible." FIFA President Gianni Infantino and US officials, including Secretary of State Marco Rubio, affirmed Iran's participation last week, clarifying athlete visas while barring IRGC-linked individuals, amid Iran's unresolved request to relocate group stage matches from US venues to Mexico. Earlier March threats of boycott have dissipated without formal withdrawal, positioning Italy (10.2%) as a speculative longshot despite diplomatic buzz, while AFC runners-up like UAE linger low amid vague replacement rules favoring continental peers.
